problems.title: Sum a List
problems.description:
Given a list of integers, return the sum of all of its elements.

**Example:**

```
Input: [1, 2, 3, 4]
Output: 10
```

**Constraints:**

- The list may be empty (the sum of an empty list is 0).
problems.core_idea: Keep a running total and add each element in a single pass. O(n) time, O(1) space.
hints: Start the accumulator at 0 so an empty list returns 0.

solutions.title: One pass accumulator

problems.title: Reverse a String
problems.description:
Return a new string with the characters of the input string in reverse order.

**Example:**

```
Input: "flashcards"
Output: "sdrachsalf"
```
problems.core_idea: Walk the string from the end to the start (or swap characters with two pointers moving inward). O(n) time.
hints: Two pointers from both ends swapping inward also works in place.

solutions.title: Two pointers

problems.title: FizzBuzz
problems.description:
For each number from 1 to n, output:

- "FizzBuzz" if it is divisible by both 3 and 5,
- "Fizz" if divisible by 3,
- "Buzz" if divisible by 5,
- otherwise the number itself.

**Example (n = 5):**

```
1, 2, Fizz, 4, Buzz
```
problems.core_idea: Check divisibility by 15 first (3 and 5), then 3, then 5, else the number. Building the string from 'Fizz'/'Buzz' parts avoids the nested checks. O(n) time.
hints: Checking divisibility by 15 before 3 and 5 keeps the branches simple.

solutions.title: Build from parts
